title: Cannabis plant production methods
abstract: A method (100) for mass production of uniform cannabis primordia comprising steps of excising axillary bud portions explants from a selected elite cannabis plant (110) encapsulating explants in sodium alginate forming beads and transferring said beads to soil wherein said steps of encapsulation comprises inoculating said explants in sterilized MS media to provide an inoculate, subculturing said inoculate and placing in vermiculite within a storage receptacle maintaining said receptacle for up to 3 weeks at a predetermined temperature and RH.
claims: Claims 1. A method 100 for mass production of uniform cannabis primordia comprising steps of excising axillary bud portions explants from a selected elite cannabis plant 110 encapsulating explants in sodium alginate forming beads 120 incubating beads in a flask 130 and transferring said beads to soil wherein said steps of encapsulation comprises inoculating said explants in sterilized MS media to provide an inoculate, subculturing said inoculate in presterilized MS for a predetermined period, providing a sodium alginate solution inoculate by adding non- autoclaved to 4% w/v Sodium alginate to said inoculate or said subculture to 4% w/v supplementing said inoculate or subculture with 0.5 mM TDZ, 2.5 mM IBA and a plant preservative to provide a solution of primordial explants in supplemented sodium alginate dropping said primordial explants mixed with bead- forming solution into a CaCL2.2H20 solution, agitating gently to provide alginate beads containing entrapped primordial explants to provide encapsulated explant beads complexing said beads with said CaCL2.2H20 solution for 30 minutes retrieving beads from solution rinsing said beads placing said beads in vermiculite within a storage receptacle maintaining said receptacle for up to 3 weeks at a predetermined temperature and RH. 2. The method according to claim 1 wherein said storage receptacle is a clear LDPE bag. 3. The method according to claim 1 wherein said LDPE or other plastic transparent or substantially transparent bag. 4. The method according to claim 1 wherein said storage receptacle is sufficiently open as to essentially equilibrate with ambient temperature, pressure and RH. 5. The method according to claim 1 wherein said ambient temperature is approx. 24C. and 60% RH 6. The method according to claim 3 wherein said clear LDPE bag is exposed to a photo period regime under fluorescent light with a photon flux of 52 m mol m-2 s-1 . 7. The method according to claim 3 wherein said storage receptacle is a closable pot with a vermiculite floor of approximately 2cm in depth. 8. The method according to claim 6 comprising steps of sowing said vermiculite floor of said pot with beads of 0.5 m m-0 .8 mm at a density of 2.5 beads per sq. cm. 9. The method according to claim 1 wherein said germination rate is at least 95%. 10. The method of the present invention wherein at least one elite reference strain of Table 1 maintains its characteristic CBD:THC ratio after sowing said beads, said beads having been stored for 20 days. 11. The method of the present invention wherein at least one elite reference strain of Table 1 maintains its characteristic CBD:THC ratio after sowing said beads, said beads having been stored for 20-30 days. 12. The method of the present invention wherein at least one elite reference strains of Table 1 maintains its characteristic CBD:THC ratio after sowing said beads, said beads having been stored for 25-45 days. 13. The method of the present invention wherein at least one elite reference strains of Table 1 maintains its characteristic CBD:THC ratio after sowing said beads, said beads having been stored for 45 days. 14. A container, package or sachet containing viable explant beads of cannabis obtained by the method of claim 1 -13 wherein said germination rate is at least 85- 95% when said beads are sown in nursery, greenhouse or field. 15. The container, package or sachet containing viable explant beads of cannabis obtained by the method of claim 1 - 13 wherein said germination rate is at least 85- 95% when said beads are sown in nursery, greenhouse or field. 16. The container, package or sachet containing viable explant beads of cannabis obtained by the method of claim 1 -13 wherein said plants attain a height of 14-16 cm in height within 5 weeks when said beads are sown in nursery, greenhouse or field.
